summary: Iteration 3: Log Incident feature fully verified. Backend 24/24 pytest pass (5 new tests for /api/incidents/structure: 401 unauth, 400 short transcript, 200 happy-path returning all 4 fields with '1)' & '6)' Ofsted sections, new incident fields (tags/structured_report/raw_transcript/incident_type) round-trip via POST->GET /api/incidents, backward compat with legacy incidents defaulting tags=[] and strings=''). Frontend Playwright at mobile 390px: Dashboard hero-log-incident and card-incidents both navigate to /incidents/new; LogIncident page (data-testid=log-incident-page) renders with all step testids (quick-resident-{id}, resident-select fallback, type-behaviour/safeguarding/absconding/other, transcript-input, structure-ai-btn, severity-low/medium/high, tag-aggression etc, safeguarding-flag, save-incident-btn, voice-record-btn); save button disabled until resident selected; type=safeguarding auto-hides/auto-checks the safeguarding-flag and switches tag chips to safeguarding set; type switch back to behaviour restores behaviour tags and exposes safeguarding-flag; structure-ai-btn is disabled on empty transcript (prevents API call — satisfies spec intent even though disabled path differs from toast path); clicking structure with filled transcript hits /api/incidents/structure and renders structured-preview with Ofsted '1) Summary …' content; save posts to /api/incidents and navigates to /incidents; no horizontal overflow at 390px (scrollWidth=clientWidth=390); sticky save bar visible (bottom=832 of 844 viewport).

issues: Minor: the 'Made with Emergent' attribution badge overlaps the sticky save bar bottom area in the preview iframe — not a code bug, but the save button sits right against the viewport bottom edge (bottom=832 / vh=844). Consider adding 8-12px extra bottom padding on the sticky container for breathing room on small devices.

critical_code_review_comments: LogIncident.jsx: spec says 'Clicking Structure with AI on empty transcript shows toast error and does not call API', but the button is disabled (aria=disabled) when transcript is empty, so the toast-error branch in structureWithAI() is unreachable. Either drop the disabled flag to let the toast path fire, or remove the dead toast line. Functional outcome (no API call) is preserved, so this is cosmetic/dead-code only.

LogIncident.jsx: structureWithAI guards 'Select a young person first' via toast, but the Structure button is NOT disabled on missing resident_id — minor UX inconsistency with the save-button handling.

LogIncident.jsx line ~65 useEffect depends only on form.incident_type (eslint-disabled) — the filter on f.tags uses TAG_SETS[f.incident_type] inside a setForm(f=>) which mixes reactive and functional updates; low risk but flagged.
Backend /api/incidents/structure: response time ~5-15s (GPT-5.2). Consider a 20-30s client timeout guard and a clearer user-facing error if the upstream LLM returns 5xx.

server.py IncidentIn: new optional fields default to empty string for structured_report/raw_transcript, which is correct for backward compat but mongo docs created before iter-3 won't have the keys — Pydantic defaults cover this on read (verified). ✓
Still-open items from iter 1/2: deprecated FastAPI on_event, raw exception text leaked from /voice/transcribe & /reports/generate, no brute-force login lockout (per playbook).

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all LogIncident flows + nav + mobile + AI structure + save all pass)

seed_data_creation: Adds TEST_Resident_Seed (session fixture), TEST_structured_incident via POST /api/incidents with all new fields. Existing teardown removes TEST_* residents.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (24 tests, ~25s). TestIncidentStructure is the iter-3 addition. JWT localStorage key: cc_token. GPT-5.2 structuring takes 5-15s — use timeout>=30s. The spec's disabled-vs-toast behavior on structure-ai-btn w/ empty transcript is cosmetic only (see code review). Dashboard hero-log-incident + card-incidents both route to /incidents/new now. Quick chips shown for first 6 residents; resident-select dropdown appears as fallback when >6.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. All iteration-3 acceptance criteria met (18/18 request items verified).
